meta.structure_note: All color custom properties in this codebase live in exactly one file (frontend/app/globals.css), declared twice each: once in :root (light theme) and once in [data-theme="dark"] (dark theme overrides). There are two parallel, coexisting palettes rather than one: a 'core' set (--color-*, the original app-wide primitives used in chat/login/pricing/shared badges) and an 'admin' set (--admin-*, a second palette ported wholesale from a separate 'Theke Admin design' prototype and layered on top of the core set for the sidebar, top bar, and every admin-facing panel/dashboard). This file preserves that real split (tokens.core / tokens.admin / tokens.chat) instead of forcing everything into a single flat list. A few tokens are intentionally NOT included below because they are not CSS custom properties at all - see 'excluded_non_variable_colors'.

excluded_non_variable_colors.note: The Logo/LogoMark brand-mark green. Hardcoded on purpose per an explicit code comment ('Colors are fixed per the brand spec regardless of light/dark theme') - it is not a CSS variable and editing this JSON will not change it. If a rebrand ever needs to touch the logo mark itself, it must be edited directly in Logo.tsx.

Map pin marker colors, duplicated as plain JS string literals because Leaflet's divIcon needs a literal SVG markup string, not a CSS var(). Currently equal to --admin-navy/--color-primary and --admin-warning respectively, but nothing enforces that - a rebrand that changes those tokens will silently leave map pins on the old colors.

Browser-chrome theme-color meta tag, duplicated as a literal because Next.js metadata is evaluated at build time. Only has a light-mode value - the OS/browser chrome (e.g. mobile status bar) does not follow the app into dark mode.

meta.instructions: Edit only the 'value' fields below. Do not rename keys, remove entries, or change structure. Return this same file with updated hex values.

dark.usage: Page background behind every card/surface
